question: What evidence supports preemptive TEVAR for uncomplicated acute or subacute type B aortic dissection?
scope_note: Original-PDF extraction from AES-RCT-001, AES-RCT-002, and AES-RCT-003 only. This is not a clinical recommendation or generated answer.

identity_outcomes.warning: Core identity matches. The printed PDF byline and indexed metadata differ regarding Sievers HH; no authorship-credit determination is made.
The publisher PDF includes an appended trialist-name correction; the registered original article is present.

draft_findings.finding: The two-year INSTEAD report did not demonstrate a significant survival or composite clinical-event advantage, although remodeling favored TEVAR.

INSTEAD-XL reported lower five-year aorta-specific mortality and disease progression after TEVAR, while the overall all-cause mortality comparison was not significant and late landmark analyses have observational limitations.
ADSORB reported improved one-year false-lumen thrombosis and remodeling measures in acute uncomplicated dissection but was not powered for mortality.
